task_completion.description: The DICOM image containing the lesion is loaded and visible in the viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A medical imaging viewer (e.g., Weasis) is open, displaying a CT scan with a bright, irregular lobulated lesion in the center.

task_completion.description: The polygon or freehand ROI measurement tool is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The polygon, freehand, or area measurement tool is highlighted or selected in the application's toolbar or menu.

task_completion.description: The irregular boundary of the central lesion is accurately traced.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A drawn polygon or freehand shape closely outlines the edges of the bright, irregular lobulated lesion in the center of the image.

task_completion.description: The area measurement of the traced lesion is displayed on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numerical area value (e.g., in cm² or mm²) is visible on the image, connected to or located near the drawn polygon.

task_completion.description: The annotated view is exported to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog shows the destination path '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/polygon_area.png', or a terminal/file manager window confirms the existence of 'polygon_area.png' in the '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual DICOM viewer GUI to perform the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the DICOM viewer interface (menus, toolbars, image viewport) being actively used, rather than terminal commands bypassing the UI to generate the image.

integrity.description: The measurement overlay is generated by the DICOM software, not faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The polygon and area text match the standard rendering style of the DICOM viewer's measurement tools, without signs of being pasted from an image editor or text editor.

integrity.description: No external image editing software was used to fake the annotation.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no image editors (like GIMP or Pinta) open in the screenshots being used to draw lines or text over the DICOM image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected export path for the annotated image is exactly '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/polygon_area.png'. The exported file must be larger than 5000 bytes. The target lesion is a bright, irregular lobulated shape located in the center of the CT image.
